title: ERM++: An Improved Baseline for Domain Generalization
key_contribution: ERM++ (Empirical Risk Minimization++), an improved baseline for domain generalization that outperforms state-of-the-art methods without requiring domain labels, architecture changes, or complex training strategies

methodology.approach: Critical evaluation of training pipeline components
themes: How training data is used (including training length and checkpoint selection)
How network parameters are initialized (pretraining network selection, fine-tuning or freezing layers)

key_findings: Improved performance on ImageNet does not necessarily lead to better generalization ability
Many methods evaluate models before they have converged
ERM++ uses an adaptive training procedure to determine sufficient training length
